summary: Molar concentration (molarity) is the amount of solute per litre of solution: c = n / V. Rearrange it to find the concentration, the amount of substance, or the volume.
method: Identify which two of c, n, V you are given.
Make sure the amount n is in moles and the volume V is in litres.
Substitute into c = n / V (or its rearrangement) and solve.
